item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: You had me until the end, the dialogue was melodramatic and didn’t fit in tonally with what had come before. Which is a shame because up until that point I really liked it. I think toning that down and maybe having a log before building up to it a little more would fix my issues with this one.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: You can have high clearance for a specific SCP without having a high general clearance.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Why would Only 13 people in the whole world have access to the full info on this SCP? No info in this should be that classified, it should be a level 3 at the most. It’s really not that important of a SCP.

And if you seriously want to pretend it’s important enough that only 05 get to hear about it atleast toss in an addendum that says the interviewer was amnesticized afterwards, they’re not 05 so they shouldn’t be allowed to remember if it truly can only be known by 05

Also why didn’t the interviewer ask her how she former a reality altering anomaly, this is the SCP foundation we don’t really care who dun it we care how they dun it. It seems like you had just the twist in your head and you rushed every other aspect of the article.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Did you create the image of this SCP?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Only cause Kazato and Kogoro are similar enough that I felt like people could get mixed up, so I instead used Akechi.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Is there a reason why the Japanese name order differs between the two characters?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: BIG +1. This is great, even just the main skip on its own is quite cool, and the murder makes it that much more compelling. I'll have a look at your author page and read some more.

item.title: Author Post
item.content:encoded:
│JAMCON 2020│ Conceptualized and posted in about 4 hours!

Because Travis Carter's death wasn't a suicide. It was a very, very well planned murder. And you're the one who committed it.

I've always wanted to write a mystery, so I did a shortform one! Completely inspired by Detective Conan/Case Closed, one of my favorite anime ever.
